question.text: Which of the following statements about time value of an option is INCORRECT?

answer.text: Time value increases as the option approaches expiration
ATM options have the highest time value
At expiration, time value is zero

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Time value DECREASES (decays) as expiration approaches, not increases. This is called time decay or theta decay. **The Trap:** The other three statements are all correct -- ATM options have highest time value, it is never negative, and it is zero at expiry. **Cross-Exam Context:** Time decay is a fundamental concept tested in all NISM derivative modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Time decay accelerates as expiry nears. Option sellers benefit from time decay while buyers suffer from it -- this is why option selling is sometimes called 'collecting theta'.
